seo.title: RabbitMQ Multi-Server Monitoring — Workspaces & Team Access | Qarote
seo.description: Connect multiple RabbitMQ instances to one Qarote workspace. Monitor production, staging, and development from a single dashboard. Team access control, TLS connections, and workspace isolation — no per-server tooling.

hero.title: One workspace. All your RabbitMQ servers.
hero.subtitle: Connect production, staging, and development brokers to a single Qarote workspace. Switch between servers in one click. Share the same dashboard with your whole team — with role-based access that keeps production operations controlled.

capabilities.heading: Everything you need for multi-environment RabbitMQ

items.title: Multiple server connections
items.description: Connect up to 3 RabbitMQ servers on the Developer plan, unlimited on Enterprise. Each server is monitored independently — separate queue lists, separate health states, separate alert configurations.

items.title: Cross-server dashboard
items.description: A unified server list shows all connected brokers with their health status at a glance. Critical servers surface at the top. Switch into any server's detail view in one click — no tab juggling, no separate browser bookmarks.

items.title: Environment isolation
items.description: Production, staging, and development are separate server entries with separate configurations. Alerts for staging don't fire in your production Slack channel. Queue management operations on development can't accidentally target production.

items.title: TLS encrypted connections
items.description: All connections to your RabbitMQ management API are over TLS. Qarote never stores your RabbitMQ credentials in plaintext — connections are tested on entry and credentials are encrypted at rest.

items.title: Team access control
items.description: Invite team members with scoped roles: Admin (full access), Member (monitoring + alerting), Viewer (read-only). Control who can manage queues, configure alerts, and connect new servers — per workspace.

items.title: Workspace organization
items.description: Workspaces separate environments at the organizational level — useful for agencies managing multiple clients' brokers, or enterprises with isolated business units. Each workspace has independent servers, users, alerts, and billing.

steps.title: Add a server
steps.description: Enter your RabbitMQ management API URL and credentials. Qarote tests the connection immediately and adds the server to your workspace. The full queue list appears within seconds.

steps.title: All environments in one view
steps.description: Switch between servers from the top navigation. Each server has its own queue list, analytics, and alert configuration. The unified health view shows all servers at once — healthy, warning, or critical.

steps.title: Invite your team
steps.description: Add team members by email. Assign roles that match their responsibilities. On-call engineers get alert access. Developers get read access. Admins manage server connections and workspace settings.

teamWorkspaces.heading: Shared context without shared risk
teamWorkspaces.p1: When your team grows beyond one person managing RabbitMQ, you have a coordination problem. Everyone bookmarks the management plugin directly. Credentials get shared over Slack. There's no record of who purged which queue. Alerts fire to a shared email alias that nobody checks.
teamWorkspaces.p2: Qarote workspaces give your team a single shared context: the same server list, the same queue dashboard, the same alert history — with individual logins and role-based access. Operations that touch production are controlled by Qarote's permission model, not by who has the SSH key.
teamWorkspaces.p3: Enterprise workspaces add SSO (OIDC/SAML) and team-level access controls. Connect your identity provider, and Qarote user management follows your existing offboarding process. When a team member leaves, revoking their Qarote access revokes their RabbitMQ monitoring access automatically.

items.question: How many servers can I connect?
items.answer: Developer plans support up to 3 RabbitMQ server connections per workspace. Enterprise plans support unlimited connections. The Community (free) edition supports 1 server.

items.question: Can I connect servers across different environments with the same workspace?
items.answer: Yes — connecting production, staging, and development to the same workspace is the intended use case. Alert configurations, user roles, and queue management permissions are all server-scoped, so environment isolation is maintained.

items.question: Does Qarote require network access from the broker to Qarote?
items.answer: Qarote connects outbound to your RabbitMQ management API (default port 15672) over HTTPS. Your broker does not need inbound network access from Qarote — only the RabbitMQ management API endpoint needs to be reachable from your Qarote installation.

items.question: How does team access work for self-hosted Qarote?
items.answer: Self-hosted Qarote uses the same role-based access model. You manage users through the Qarote admin panel. Enterprise self-hosted adds OIDC/SAML SSO — connect your identity provider and user provisioning follows your existing directory.

items.question: Can different team members have different access per server?
items.answer: In the current version, roles are workspace-scoped — a Member can see all servers in the workspace. Server-scoped permissions (per-server read/write control) are on the Enterprise roadmap.
